evals.name: should-trigger-transition-via-cli
evals.prompt: Use acli to transition UPEX-123 to In Review now that the PR is open.
evals.expected_behavior: Activates acli skill. Reads SKILL.md for the transition section, then references/workitem.md §transition. Runs `acli jira workitem transition --key UPEX-123 --status "{{jira.status.story.in_review}}"`. If the user has not authenticated, suggests `acli jira auth status` first and points at references/auth.md. Does NOT route to /sprint-development — the user explicitly asked for a CLI-only operation.

evals.name: should-trigger-create-bug-from-cli
evals.prompt: Create a Bug from acli — title 'Login button does nothing on Safari 17', parent UPEX-123, project UPEX, assign to me.
evals.expected_behavior: Activates acli skill. Loads references/workitem.md §create. Runs `acli jira workitem create --project UPEX --type Bug --summary "Login button does nothing on Safari 17" --parent UPEX-123 --assignee "@me"`. Reports the new bug key. Does NOT use Test/Test-Execution issue types (those are QA-side and not in scope for this skill).

evals.name: should-trigger-fetch-story-details
evals.prompt: Pull UPEX-277 from Jira via acli — I need the ACs, scope and mockup link to write the impl plan.
evals.expected_behavior: Activates acli skill. Loads references/workitem.md §view. Runs `acli jira workitem view UPEX-277 --json` and pipes through jq to extract the relevant custom fields (acceptance_criteria, scope, mockup, business_rules_specification, workflow). References fields by slug per the boilerplate convention ({{jira.<slug>}}) and notes that the actual customfield IDs come from .agents/jira-fields.json.

evals.name: should-trigger-bulk-transition-jql
evals.prompt: Use acli to bulk-transition every UPEX story that has fixVersion 2026.05 and is in Ready For QA over to Deployed To Production.
evals.expected_behavior: Activates acli skill. Reads SKILL.md selector pattern + references/workitem.md §transition. Runs `acli jira workitem transition --jql "project = UPEX AND fixVersion = '2026.05' AND status = '{{jira.status.story.ready_for_qa}}'" --status "{{jira.status.story.deployed_to_production}}" --yes --ignore-errors`. Warns about silent pagination and rate limits per references/gotchas.md (#1, #16). Suggests the dry-run preview pattern from references/output-and-automation.md before mutating.

evals.name: should-trigger-spanish-acli-trigger
evals.prompt: Necesito loguearme en acli para mi sitio de Atlassian — paso el token por env, ¿cómo es?
evals.expected_behavior: Activates acli skill. Mirrors Spanish in conversation. Loads references/auth.md. Explains the API-token path: `echo "$ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN" | acli jira auth login --site "${ATLASSIAN_URL#https://}" --email "$ATLASSIAN_EMAIL" --token`. Mentions the `.env` keys this boilerplate uses (ATLASSIAN_URL, ATLASSIAN_EMAIL, ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN — single credential family, no JIRA_* overrides). Notes that the site slug is derived from ATLASSIAN_URL by stripping the https:// prefix, and that --token reads only from stdin (pipe/redirect/here-string).

evals.name: should-not-trigger-playwright-tests
evals.prompt: Write Playwright E2E tests for the login flow.
evals.expected_behavior: Does NOT activate acli skill. Should route to /playwright-cli (or playwright-best-practices) because this is browser-automation / E2E testing, not Jira CLI work. acli is the issue-tracker CLI — it does not write or run tests.

evals.name: should-not-trigger-full-sprint-development-loop
evals.prompt: Implement UPEX-277 — full sprint loop, plan to code to review to deploy.
evals.expected_behavior: Does NOT activate acli skill as the entry point. Should route to /sprint-development because this is full per-story orchestration. /sprint-development will internally call acli for the Jira transitions (Ready For Dev -> In Progress -> In Review -> Ready For QA), but the user-facing entry point is the orchestrator, not the CLI primitive.

evals.name: should-not-trigger-product-backlog-seed
evals.prompt: Seed the backlog with 30 stories from the PRD — refine ACs, INVEST check, ready-for-dev checklist.
evals.expected_behavior: Does NOT activate acli skill as the entry point. Should route to /product-management because this is backlog seeding + AC refinement, not raw CLI work. /product-management may delegate the bulk-create step to acli (`workitem create-bulk --from-csv`), but the orchestration, INVEST check, and Gherkin authoring belong to the product-management workflow.
